experiment.description: Characterises the bits-mode DL context effect: a proposal's delta_bits is GLOBAL (depends on entire symbol table), so accepts that amortise not/1 and exception functors discount later proposals. Analysis A: order-robustness of the noise filter (3 orderings x L noise groups). Analysis B: amortisation discount magnitude isolated analytically (fixed N=3+1exc proposal, k prior exception structures). Analysis C: scope statement derived from A+B. Symbolic only, no LLM.
experiment.name: global-DL context effect

params.analysis_A_design: EX32 noisy domain, 3 orderings x L noise groups in {1..5}. bits mode dream, count spurious Op C accepts.
params.analysis_B_design: Fixed N=3+1exc proposal against KB with k prior exception structures (k in {0..4}). Measures amortisation discount analytically.
params.ex29_reference: N=3 with 1 exc: delta_clauses=-6 (raw), delta_bits~+6 on lean KB (bits rejects). N=4+: both accept.
